Is square e7 NOT highlighted (it is occupied by a black piece and thus not a legal destination)?
Are no other squares besides a5, b4, b8, d4, and e5 highlighted as destinations for the knight on c6?

Board (Go).text: A Tesuji problem. Black to play. Black must rescue the two stones. Please find the crucial first move and mark it with "1" on the board.

questions.question: Is there exactly one move marker labeled "1" added to the board?
Is the move marked with "1" placed at the intersection Q18?
Is the stone at Q18 shown as a black stone (indicating Black's first move)?
Is the numeral "1" clearly on the black stone at Q18, not elsewhere on the board?

Board (Chinese Chess).text: Highlight the legal moves of the Red left rook.

questions.question: Are all highlighted squares for the Red left rook located strictly along its rank or file (no diagonal highlights)?
Are there no highlighted squares that lie beyond any piece along the rook’s lines of movement (i.e., no path passes through another piece)?
Are there no highlighted squares that are occupied by another Red piece (friendly piece squares are not selectable)?
Is the rightward direction from the Red left rook unhighlighted because it is immediately blocked by the adjacent Red right rook?
Are leftward squares along the same rank highlighted continuously up to the board edge or until a piece is met?
Are upward squares from the Red left rook highlighted only up to the edge of the board or until the first encountered piece, with no dots beyond that point?
Are downward squares from the Red left rook highlighted continuously along the same file until the first encountered piece, with no dots beyond that blocker?
Are there no move highlights originating from the Red right rook or any other piece (i.e., only the left rook’s legal moves are shown)?
